title: Visualise Agent Skill
description: Explore how this agent skill enables coding agents to render rich SVG diagrams, interactive HTML widgets, and data charts inline in conversations
prerequisites: Basic understanding of agent skills and LLM tool use

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: Visualise is an agent skill that lets coding agents generate rich visual content — SVG diagrams, HTML widgets, and Chart.js charts — rendered inline in conversations. The entire project is a documentation and instruction system with no build step or runtime code.

steps.title: Skill Entry Point
steps.description: SKILL.md is the agent-facing entry point. When an agent loads this skill, it reads SKILL.md first (~100 tokens) to learn activation triggers (visualize, diagram, chart, draw), output constraints (sandboxed iframe, streaming order), and which reference docs to load on demand. This progressive-disclosure pattern keeps token cost low.

steps.title: Reference Library
steps.description: The references/ directory contains progressive-disclosure docs that agents load only when needed. design-system.md provides CSS variables and color ramps. diagrams.md covers flowcharts and structural visuals. components.md has interactive HTML patterns. charts.md handles Chart.js and D3 integration. client-implementation.md is the renderer setup guide.

steps.title: Design System
steps.description: The design system defines the visual language: 9 color ramps with 7 intensity stops each, CSS variables for theming, pre-built SVG classes, and strict rules — flat design, no gradients, 680 px viewBox, 0.5px strokes. This ensures all agent-generated visuals look native and consistent.

steps.title: How It All Connects
steps.description: The execution flow: (1) Agent reads SKILL.md at startup. (2) User asks to visualize something. (3) Agent loads only the needed reference — design-system.md always, plus diagrams/components/charts as required. (4) Agent generates HTML/SVG in a ```visualizer code fence. (5) Client renderer detects the fence, injects theme CSS, renders in a sandboxed iframe. (6) Interactive elements can trigger follow-up messages via sendPrompt.
